raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites judgment on the thesis, claimed gap, and contribution framing of the proposed method.","R1.2":"Assessing what to think of the passage centrally involves whether the theoretical argument and conclusion chain are coherent.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical and theoretical claims that would need to be checked against the reported evidence within the text.","R1.4":"The passage contains an explicit first-method novelty claim, making novelty assessment central to the requested reaction.","R1.5":"A general reaction to a research passage includes whether the abstract-like narrative and ordering support the contribution effectively.","R1.6":"The delegation naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level readability, concision, and academic register judgments.","R1.7":"Because the artifact relies on named prior work and theorem attribution, citation and scholarship responsibility is active.","RX.1":"Any projection of an opinion-style review must disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks full paper context or external verification.","RX.2":"The response must guard against endorsing or amplifying claims such as novelty and guarantees beyond what the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually critiquing or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use of the research passage.","RX.5":"The responsibility assessment should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact, such as novelty, citations, and evidence claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites judgment on the thesis, claimed gap, and contribution framing of the proposed method.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what to think of the passage centrally involves whether the theoretical argument and conclusion chain are coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical and theoretical claims that would need to be checked against the reported evidence within the text.
rationale.R1.4: The passage contains an explicit first-method novelty claim, making novelty assessment central to the requested reaction.
rationale.R1.5: A general reaction to a research passage includes whether the abstract-like narrative and ordering support the contribution effectively.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level readability, concision, and academic register judgments.
rationale.R1.7: Because the artifact relies on named prior work and theorem attribution, citation and scholarship responsibility is active.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an opinion-style review must disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks full paper context or external verification.
rationale.RX.2: The response must guard against endorsing or amplifying claims such as novelty and guarantees beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually critiquing or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use of the research passage.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility assessment should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact, such as novelty, citations, and evidence claims.
